vulnerability.reason: Reference describes vulnerability as open benchmark datasets enabling mass dissemination of filter-evasion techniques; generated describes user misplaced trust in limited benchmarks leading to inability to recognize circumventing prompts – a different sensitivity condition.
vulnerability.reference: Benchmarking datasets without strict access controls enable mass dissemination of filter-evasion techniques.
vulnerability.generated: Users trust safety mechanisms validated only on limited benchmarks, leaving them unable to recognize prompts that circumvent content filters.

impact.reason: Both reference and generated mention public exposure to NSFW/objectionable images. However, the specific damage type of reinforcing harmful gender/racial stereotypes and normalizing discrimination is absent from the generated text.
impact.reference: Public exposure to NSFW imagery reinforcing harmful gender and racial stereotypes, normalizing discrimination.
impact.generated: NSFW and copyrighted images proliferate publicly via text-to-image services, bypassing all deployed safeguards.

dose_response.reason: Reference causal chain: automated discovery → bypass NSFW controls → via leaked prompts from benchmarks → exposes users to stereotyping NSFW imagery. Generated chain: automated discovery → through users triggers misplaced trust in benchmarks → leads to proliferation of NSFW/copyrighted images and bypassing safeguards. The mechanisms differ fundamentally.
dose_response.reference: Automated discovery of prompt-level vulnerabilities enables bypass of NSFW controls, which, via leaked adversarial prompts from safety benchmarks, exposes online users to pervasive NSFW imagery that reinforces harmful stereotypes and normalizes discrimination.
dose_response.generated: Automated discovery of prompt-level vulnerabilities in T2I safety mechanisms, through online users, triggers misplaced trust in limited safety benchmarks, leading to NSFW and copyrighted images proliferating and bypassing safeguards.
